Both ETFs track the Nasdaq 100 Index, which has a significant allocation to tech stocks, and analysts are bullish on the sector for the remainder of 2021 following the Federal Reserve's recent messaging. QQQM offers a lower expense ratio of 0.15% compared to QQQ's 0.20%, positioning it well to benefit from renewed investor interest in growth stocks like Apple and Microsoft.

feed.title: Zimmer Biomet Announces New Data Supporting Clinical Benefits of mymobility® with Apple Watch® at AAOS 2021
feed.summary: Zimmer Biomet announced new data at the AAOS 2021 Annual Meeting supporting the clinical benefits of its mymobility® with Apple Watch® remote care management platform. Four studies highlighted that the platform can effectively support patients recovering from knee and hip replacement surgeries. The data suggest that mymobility with Apple Watch provides similar outcomes to traditional care, potentially reduces healthcare utilization, and offers valuable passively collected physical activity data for monitoring recovery.

feed.title: Get Ready To Use Your iPhone As ID At The Airport
feed.summary: Apple's digital ID initiative, allowing users to add their driver's license or state ID to the Wallet app, is launching soon. Arizona and Georgia will be the first states to accept these digital IDs at TSA checkpoints, with other states to follow. This privacy-focused feature aims to simplify identity verification for air travel and other transactions, using biometric authentication for security.

feed.title: Financial services giant Charles Schwab to add 450 jobs in Austin
feed.summary: Charles Schwab, a major financial services company, announced an expansion in Austin that will create approximately 450 new jobs across various functions, including software development, engineering, and client services. This expansion is part of the company's significant growth in 2021, which saw record net new assets and brokerage accounts, and follows a company-wide 5% pay increase for employees. Schwab joins several other large corporations like Oracle, Apple, Amazon, Facebook, and Google in expanding their presence and job offerings in Austin, leveraging the city's tech talent pool.

feed.title: Salt adds Universal remote to Apple TV service
feed.summary: Swiss telco Salt has integrated a Universal Electronics-produced Apple TV remote into its service, designed specifically for Multichannel Video Program Distributors (MVPDs). The remote, launched by Salt in June, combines Bluetooth Low Energy and Infrared for universal control and is also available as a standalone accessory. This collaboration enhances the TV viewing experience for Salt's Apple TV 4K IPTV service customers.

feed.title: Apple offers small concession in easing App Store rules for Netflix, others
feed.summary: Apple announced a concession through a settlement with Japan's anti-trust regulator, allowing "reader" apps like Netflix and Spotify to link to their websites for user payments, bypassing Apple's 30% App Store fee. This change, effective early next year, aims to address some antitrust concerns but has been criticized by companies like Spotify and Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney as insufficient, particularly regarding game developers and broader app store policies. The move comes amid increasing global scrutiny and other recent challenges to Apple's App Store rules across various countries.

feed.title: Broadcom forecasts upbeat current-quarter sales on 5G bet
feed.summary: Broadcom Inc. forecasted fourth-quarter revenue above Wall Street expectations, driven by strong demand for semiconductors due to 5G adoption and hybrid work models. The company's wireless division, including Apple as a major customer, is also expected to benefit from new iPhone launches. Broadcom's diversification into software business has helped mitigate supply chain disruptions.

feed.title: Wearable Devices Market Trend, Growth, Detailed Analysis
feed.summary: The wearable devices market is projected to grow at an impressive CAGR of 18.78% from 2021 to 2028, driven by the increasing penetration of smartphones, internet connectivity, and rising consumer awareness toward physical fitness. Key players in this market include SAMSUNG, Apple Inc., Garmin Ltd., Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd., and Sony Corporation. The market analysis covers various segments such as wrist-wear, eyewear, footwear, and different types of smart textiles, with detailed country-level insights and a look at the impact of COVID-19.

feed.title: Globalstar (GSAT) Stock Forecast 2025—Why It's a Good Investment
feed.summary: Globalstar (GSAT) stock has surged over 500% in 2021 due to rumors of an Apple partnership for satellite communications in the new iPhone 13. While analysts have varying target prices, the company is seen as a good investment due to potential revenue increases from an Apple deal and the growing demand for satellite services, despite competition from Starlink and AST SpaceMobile.

feed.title: Toll Brothers plans another high-end neighborhood in Austin area
feed.summary: Toll Brothers, the nation's 11th-largest homebuilder, is planning another high-end neighborhood in Leander, north of Austin. The company is targeting 112 acres to build 330 luxury homes, with half-million-dollar homes expected to be the focus. This development continues the trend of rapid growth in the Leander area.

feed.title: College debt crisis drives more students away from four-year schools
feed.summary: The rising cost of college and the ongoing debt crisis are pushing more students away from traditional four-year universities towards more affordable alternatives like coding boot camps, community colleges, and trade schools. Companies like Apple and Google are also reducing their reliance on college degrees, making alternative education more appealing. Surveys show a significant drop in high school students planning to attend four-year schools, with many prioritizing career training and financial viability.

feed.title: Garmin Venu 2S review: Stylish fitness watch packed with features
feed.summary: The Garmin Venu 2S is reviewed as a stylish fitness watch, particularly appealing to women due to its elegant design and 40mm dial. While praised for its AMOLED display, extensive fitness tracking features (including GPS, pulse oximeter, and heart rate monitoring), solid battery life, and Spotify integration, the review notes some issues with inaccurate swim distance tracking and an at times confusing user interface with notifications. Despite these minor drawbacks and its premium price of Rs 37,990, the Venu 2S is recommended for hardcore fitness enthusiasts.

feed.title: Alphabet, Tesla and Amazon are among the most favourite stocks held by US-focused mutual funds. Here are top 10 stocks
feed.summary: This article identifies Alphabet, Tesla, and Amazon as top stock holdings among U.S.-focused mutual funds available to Indian investors. It details 10 such mutual funds, highlighting their investment strategies, underlying assets, and historical performance. The funds provide Indian investors exposure to leading US companies and geographical diversification.

feed.title: Lennox announces new integration of its iComfort thermostat
feed.summary: Lennox has expanded the compatibility of its iComfort thermostats to integrate with various smart home automation platforms, including premium systems like Control4 and popular virtual assistants such as Amazon Alexa, Apple HomeKit, Google Assistant, and IFTTT. This integration allows users to manage their Lennox heating and cooling systems through a wider range of smart home devices, offering enhanced convenience and control over their indoor environment. Control4 customers with Lennox HVAC systems and iComfort S30, E30, or M30 thermostats can request integration through their dealer, enabling centralized control from their smart home system.

feed.title: S&P 500 ends down, Big Tech lifts Nasdaq to record
feed.summary: The S&P 500 closed lower on Tuesday, while the Nasdaq reached a record high, driven by Big Tech stocks amidst investor concerns about a slowing economic recovery. The article highlights that while Apple and Netflix hit record highs, broader market sectors declined due to worries about the Delta variant and lukewarm employment data. Morgan Stanley also downgraded U.S. stocks, anticipating a bumpy period ahead.

feed.title: Stryker Acquires Med Device Company Gauss Surgical
feed.summary: Stryker announced its acquisition of Gauss Surgical, a medical device company known for its AI-enabled Triton platform that monitors blood loss during surgery. The Triton system utilizes artificial intelligence and mobile technology to provide real-time, accurate blood loss measurements, which has been shown to improve maternal and surgical care by enabling earlier recognition and intervention of hemorrhage. Financial terms of the acquisition were not disclosed.

feed.title: Stryker buys Gauss Surgical for its AI tech that tracks blood loss during childbirth
feed.summary: Stryker has acquired Gauss Surgical for its AI-powered Triton system, which tracks blood loss during surgery and childbirth to help prevent postpartum hemorrhaging. The Triton system uses an iPhone app and computer vision to measure blood loss from sponges, towels, and fluid canisters, alerting caregivers to dangerous situations. Following the acquisition, Gauss's home diagnostic efforts, including a smartphone-paired rapid antigen test for COVID-19, will be spun off into a new separate company called Exa Health.

feed.title: Focal Point Names Scott Pomerantz, formerly of Global Locate and Broadcom, as CEO
feed.summary: Focal Point Positioning, a UK-based deep tech startup, has appointed Scott Pomerantz as its new CEO. Pomerantz, who founded Global Locate and later sold it to Broadcom, brings 30 years of experience in positioning and semiconductors. Focal Point's Supercorrelation technology aims to improve GPS chip performance in various devices, and Pomerantz will lead the company's efforts to scale up technical support, customer integration, and commercialization.

feed.title: รุ่นพี่ปะทะรุ่นน้อง QQQ ETF กับ QQQJ ETF
feed.summary: This article compares two Invesco ETFs, QQQ and QQQJ, which both track the NASDAQ-100 Index. QQQ invests in the top 100 large-cap technology companies on Nasdaq, while QQQJ (NASDAQ Next Gen 100 ETF) focuses on companies ranked 101-200. The article highlights differences in expense ratios, fund size, and investment diversification, noting that QQQJ has shown better one-year returns, while QQQ has performed better year-to-date, with QQQJ exhibiting higher volatility.

feed.title: PayPal deepens its buy-now-pay-later push with acquisition of Japan’s Paidy for $2.7 billion
feed.summary: PayPal has announced its acquisition of Japanese buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) startup Paidy for 300 billion yen ($2.7 billion) to strengthen its presence in the BNPL market, particularly in Japan. Paidy allows consumers to make online purchases and pay in installments at convenience stores, catering to Japan's cash-centric economy. This acquisition follows other significant BNPL deals in the industry, highlighting the growing global trend of flexible payment options.

feed.title: Find Lost Items at Home with Alexa, Google, Xfinity, and Siri
feed.summary: This article provides guidance on how to locate lost items within a home using various smart assistants such as Alexa, Google, Xfinity, and Siri. It covers different methods and tips for leveraging these technologies to find misplaced belongings.

feed.title: Apple can’t force developers to use its in-app payment system, federal judge rules
feed.summary: A federal judge ruled that Apple Inc. can no longer compel developers to use its in-app payment system, allowing them to bypass commission fees of 15% to 30%. This decision, stemming from the Epic Games Inc. case, could significantly alter how apps are distributed by dominant online platforms like Apple, Alphabet Inc. (Google), and Amazon.com Inc. However, the ruling stopped short of declaring Apple an antitrust monopolist.

feed.title: The 10 Richest People on Wall Street and How to Invest in Them
feed.summary: This article identifies the ten wealthiest individuals on Wall Street, based on Forbes' Real Time Billionaires list, and offers strategies for investors to potentially emulate their success. It suggests methods like investing in their publicly traded vehicles, analyzing their 13F SEC filings, or focusing on their core investment philosophies. The article details each billionaire's background, key holdings, and how individual investors might gain exposure to their strategies.

feed.title: Epic Games court ruling unlikely to seriously damage Apple's services business
feed.summary: A court ruling in the Epic Games case allows iPhone apps to link to external payment systems, bypassing Apple's in-app purchase cut. Despite Apple's stock drop, analysts believe the financial impact will be limited because developers cannot build alternative payment mechanisms directly into apps, and consumers may still prefer Apple's easier in-app payments. The ruling is seen as a victory for Apple as it did not challenge their right to control software on their store or declare them a monopoly, while preserving the significant profit engine of their services business.

feed.title: Single Stocks & ETFs Do Mix
feed.summary: This article discusses how single stocks and ETFs can both have a place in an investment portfolio. It highlights the similarities and differences, emphasizing the importance of considering overall portfolio concentration when adding individual stocks. The author also weighs the pros and cons, such as lower costs for single stocks versus the diversification and professional management benefits of ETFs.

feed.title: Renate Nyborg becomes Tinder’s first female CEO
feed.summary: Match Group Inc. has appointed Renate Nyborg as the new chief executive officer of Tinder, making her the dating app's first female CEO since its inception in 2012. Nyborg, who previously led Tinder's operations in Europe, Middle East, and Africa, will succeed Jim Lanzone. Her appointment is effective September 27 and she will report to Match CEO Shar Dubey.

feed.title: Apple, Google, Amazon spying on you, lawsuits claim
feed.summary: Lawsuits allege that Apple, Google, and Amazon are spying on users through their voice assistants, recording private conversations and sharing them with advertisers. A class-action lawsuit against Apple's Siri has been greenlit by a federal judge, with similar claims made against Google and Amazon. The companies deny these allegations, stating that recordings are only kept with user consent or to improve service, and offer options for users to manage their privacy settings.

feed.title: Secret to Apple’s iPhone Sales Boom: Big 5G Deals From Wireless Carriers - WSJ
feed.summary: Apple's recent surge in iPhone sales is largely attributed to aggressive 5G promotions by wireless carriers like Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile. These carriers have offered substantial subsidies to encourage customers to upgrade to 5G-capable iPhones and utilize their new high-speed networks. The article questions how long these costly carrier battles will continue to fuel iPhone sales as Apple prepares to unveil new 5G-enabled models.

feed.title: Secret to Apple’s iPhone Sales Boom: Big 5G Deals From Wireless Carriers
feed.summary: Apple's iPhone sales have seen a boom since the introduction of 5G-capable models, largely driven by aggressive promotional offers from wireless carriers. Major carriers like Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile have implemented substantial subsidies to retain customers and encourage upgrades to their 5G services. As Apple prepares to unveil new 5G iPhones, a key question remains about the sustainability of these carrier incentives in fueling iPhone sales.

feed.title: How Navi's FoF on Vanguard US Total Stock Market ETF could add value to your portfolio
feed.summary: Navi Mutual Fund has filed for a new Fund of Fund (FoF) that will invest in the Vanguard Total Stock Market Index Fund ETF (VTI), a passively managed US-based ETF with a very low expense ratio. This FoF will allow Indian investors to access the broad US equity market, including companies typically unavailable in India, potentially adding value due to low correlation with the Indian market. However, investors should be aware of debt fund taxation rules for international funds.

feed.title: Apple fixes flaw exploited by Israeli firm’s Pegasus spyware
authors: Davide Scigliuzzo and William Turton and Bloomberg
feed.summary: Apple has released software updates to patch a critical security flaw in its Messages app that was reportedly exploited by NSO Group's Pegasus spyware. This "zero-click" vulnerability allowed hackers to access devices without user interaction, affecting iPhones, iPads, Macs, and Apple Watches. The flaw was discovered by Citizen Lab, which stated it was used to target a Saudi activist's device.

feed.title: Ford hires former Lowe's, Home Depot executive as chief digital and information officer
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has appointed Mike Amend, formerly of Lowe's Companies Inc., as its new chief digital and information officer to lead the company's technology and software platforms. This strategic hire aligns with CEO Jim Farley's vision to transform Ford through electrification and advanced technologies, leveraging Amend's experience in e-commerce and digital growth from his previous roles at Lowe's, JCPenney, and Home Depot. Amend will work alongside Hau Thai-Tang and recent hire Doug Field to drive Ford's technological initiatives and develop new software solutions.

feed.title: Apple loses (and wins) rulings in 'home button' patent dispute
feed.summary: A U.S. court has affirmed Apple's win in invalidating parts of one fingerprint-authentication patent held by Korean company Firstface Co Ltd, while simultaneously upholding two other Firstface patents that Apple had challenged. The dispute centers on "home buttons" or "home keys" that wake devices and authenticate users via fingerprints. This mixed ruling comes after Firstface sued Apple and Samsung in 2018 for infringing these patents.

feed.title: Redesigned Sonos Beam supports Dolby Atmos, eARC
feed.summary: Sonos has redesigned its Beam soundbar, now in its second generation, to include Dolby Atmos support and HDMI eARC connectivity. The newer model retains the compact size of its predecessor but features a faster CPU for virtualized Dolby Atmos height effects and an updated polycarbonate grille. Shipping October 5 for $449, it will also support lossless and Dolby Atmos Music from Amazon Music.

feed.title: Apple reveals iPhone 13 lineup, iPad Mini price increase and larger Apple Watch
feed.summary: Apple Inc. unveiled its new lineup of devices for the holiday shopping season, including the iPhone 13, a more expensive iPad Mini, and a new Apple Watch with a larger screen. The announcement comes as the most valuable U.S. public company aims to focus on the busy holiday period rather than its ongoing regulatory challenges. These annual product reveals typically precede the holiday shopping frenzy.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs appoints Coleman as new CFO, replacing Scherr
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. announced that Denis Coleman will replace Stephen Scherr as Chief Financial Officer at year-end. Coleman, currently co-head of the global financing group, will assume the deputy CFO role immediately. Scherr, who has been CFO since November 2018, will remain until the end of the year and formally retire at the end of January.

feed.title: Apple’s Newest iPhone Comes With a $0 Price Tag Again at AT&T
feed.summary: AT&T (T) is offering a new iPhone 13 Pro for free to new and existing customers with qualifying trade-ins and unlimited data plans, a deal valued at up to $1,000. This move marks a second year of giveaways for the carrier, aiming to extend subscriber growth and gain market share following the debut of Apple Inc.'s (AAPL) newest phones. T-Mobile US Inc. and Verizon Communications Inc. are also offering similar competitive deals, matching or providing substantial credits towards the new iPhone 13 models.

feed.title: Here's everything Apple just announced at its iPhone 13 event
feed.summary: Apple held its fall product event, unveiling the new iPhone 13 lineup, Apple Watch Series 7, and updated iPad and iPad mini models. Key improvements across the new iPhones include smaller notches, bigger batteries, and enhanced camera features, while the new Apple Watch boasts a larger display. The event also highlighted the international expansion of Apple Fitness+ and the upcoming iOS 15 release.

feed.title: Apple offers iPhone 13 in four models
feed.summary: Apple unveiled its new iPhone 13 lineup, offering four models with modest upgrades from previous versions, including an option for 1 terabyte of storage. Despite recent controversies for Apple, the new iPhones are expected to boost sales and continue the company's strong performance, although initial investor reaction was subdued. The company also previewed its next Apple Watch.

feed.title: The rise of the customer innovation center
feed.summary: Customer innovation centers (CICs) are evolving from traditional customer experience centers (CECs) to provide more experiential and memorable B2B engagements, addressing the historical lag in B2B customer experience compared to B2C. These new CICs focus on building brand identity, generating excitement through storytelling, reimagining the "new office" for hybrid workforces, and embedding constant change through accessible communication ecosystems. With the incorporation of hybrid compatibility and AI-experience curation, CICs are poised to become a critical component of experiential marketing, pushing creativity and connectivity for brands.

feed.title: Doraville’s ‘Studio City’ developer gobbles up pioneering Third Rail Studios
feed.summary: Gray Television, the developer behind Doraville's "Studio City" project, has acquired Third Rail Studios for $27.5 million. Third Rail Studios, a 70,000-square-foot film center that opened in 2015, was the first vertical component at the former Assembly Yards site and has hosted productions for Netflix, NBC, and Warner Bros. The acquisition is part of Gray Television's plan to transform the former General Motors plant into a 127-acre "Studio City," aiming to be the largest melding of TV/film production and mixed-use development in Georgia.

feed.title: Apple reveals Apple Watch Series 7, featuring the largest, most advanced display
feed.summary: Apple announced the Apple Watch Series 7, featuring a reengineered Always-On Retina display with significantly more screen area and thinner borders, making it the largest and most advanced display ever. The new series boasts enhanced durability, faster charging, new aluminum case colors, and runs on watchOS 8. It also introduces new workout types, improved fall detection for cycling, and expands Apple Fitness+ to more countries.

feed.title: AT&T to offer all-new iPhone 13 Pro, iPhone 13 Pro Max, iPhone 13, iPhone 13 mini, iPad, and iPad mini with Orders Starting on Friday, September 17
feed.summary: AT&T announced it will offer the new iPhone 13 lineup, including the iPhone 13 Pro, Pro Max, 13, and 13 mini, alongside the 9th generation iPad and new iPad mini. Pre-orders begin September 17th, with availability on September 24th. The company highlights its 5G network and special offers, including deals for new and existing customers and services like AT&T Right To You for device setup.

feed.title: A free iPhone 13? Wireless carriers’ subsidies could make it happen, with some catches
feed.summary: Wireless carriers like AT&T, Verizon, and T-Mobile are once again offering significant discounts, potentially even a free iPhone 13, to attract new customers. This strategy, which resumed last year, aims to leverage the appeal of Apple’s new smartphones to shore up customer bases as 5G network access expands. While the iPhone 13 lineup offers few new features, the aggressive carrier subsidies are expected to drive sales.

feed.title: Apple's new iPhone 13 touts faster 5G, sharper cameras to spur trade-ins
feed.summary: Apple unveiled the iPhone 13 and a new iPad mini, featuring faster 5G, improved cameras, and a more powerful A15 Bionic chip, without increasing the phone's price. Though no revolutionary features were announced, analysts expect these upgrades, coupled with significant trade-in incentives from wireless carriers, will encourage customers to upgrade their older Apple devices. The company also updated its Apple Watch and base-model iPad, aiming to strengthen customer loyalty within its ecosystem despite recent regulatory scrutiny on its business practices.

feed.title: Apple* and John Deere* shareholders file resolutions questioning their anti-repair stances
feed.summary: Shareholders of Apple and John Deere have filed resolutions questioning the companies' anti-repair policies. Green Century Capital Management, an affiliated mutual fund company, cites concerns about anti-competitive practices, environmental sustainability, and the companies' stances creating risks for shareholders due to potential regulation and brand damage. The article highlights that despite lobbying efforts, pressure for Right to Repair reforms is growing from government and state legislatures.

feed.title: An Extremely Simple 3 ETF Portfolio to Consider
feed.summary: This article proposes a simple three-ETF portfolio for long-term investors who prefer not to engage in individual stock analysis. The suggested ETFs are the Vanguard Total Stock Market Index Fund (VTI), the Vanguard FTSE All-World ex-US Index Fund ETF Shares (VEU), and the Vanguard Total Bond Market Index Fund ETF Shares (BND). The author highlights VTI's objective to track the US stock market and details its top holdings, current price, and expense ratio.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs to acquire GreenSky for $2.24 billion
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. has agreed to acquire GreenSky Inc. for approximately $2.24 billion, integrating the fintech company into its Marcus consumer-banking platform. This acquisition will add GreenSky's payment plan services for home-improvement and healthcare needs, along with its network of over 10,000 merchants. The deal aims to expand Goldman's consumer-banking offerings and reach new customers through merchant partnerships.

feed.title: Shares of this obscure chip component supplier have surged by over 1200% in the past three years
feed.summary: Shares of Nan Ya Printed Circuit Board Corp., an obscure Taiwanese company that supplies essential Ajinomoto build-up film (ABF) substrates for chipmaking, have surged over 1200% in the past three years. This surge is due to ABF substrate becoming a critical bottleneck in the semiconductor industry, with supply expected to remain constrained until at least 2025. Major tech companies are struggling to secure enough of this component, leading to increased profits and investment for substrate manufacturers.

feed.title: Apple Pay: Usuarios logran enrolar tarjetas de crédito del Banco de Chile
feed.summary: Clients of Banco de Chile successfully enrolled their credit cards with Apple Pay by changing their device's region to the United States. This unexpected integration suggests an imminent official launch of Apple Pay in Chile, despite the bank later blocking the enrollment possibility and declining to comment. The temporary success and subsequent notification indicate a direct, albeit unofficial, link between Banco de Chile and Apple's payment platform.

feed.title: GUC supports TSMC's 3nm technology at 0.75V ...
feed.summary: Taiwanese ASIC company GUC has developed a design flow for TSMC’s N3 3nm CMOS process, partnering with EDA vendors like Synopsys, Cadence, and Siemens EDA. GUC has also completed key N3 design elements, including a Computing Engine operating at 0.75V and customized low-power cells. The company is now working on N3 test chips and porting its IP, like its 5nm GUC-Link 3D interconnect, to 3nm.

feed.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Board Approves $60B Share Repurchase Program
feed.summary: Microsoft's board has approved a new $60 billion share repurchase program and increased its quarterly dividend by 11% to 62 cents per share. This move follows a series of similar buyback programs initiated by the company over the past decade, with the latest coming as a previous $40 billion program is nearing its completion. The announcement is particularly noteworthy given recent efforts by Democrats to impose a tax on corporate stock buybacks, a practice that critics argue can artificially inflate stock prices and EPS.

feed.title: iPhone 13 T-Mobile Forever Upgrade Promo Allows You To Save Up To $800, But Here's the Catch
feed.summary: T-Mobile has introduced its "Forever Upgrade" promotion for the new iPhone 13 lineup, offering savings of up to $800 on a new iPhone 13 and $800 off future iPhones every two years. However, customers should be aware of certain conditions, including a limit of four enrollments per account, incompatibility with Free Line promotions, trade-in restrictions, and the requirement of a premium plan like Magenta Plus, ONE Plus, or Magenta/Sprint MAX. Other carriers like AT&T and Verizon are also offering deals to reduce the price of new iPhones.

feed.title: NC Fought Hard for Gilead’s New Site, Adding to Exploding Biotech Presence
feed.summary: North Carolina aggressively vied with Atlanta to secure Gilead's new business services center, offering significant incentives like a nearly $10 million reimbursement. The new facility in the Research Triangle will create 275 high-paying HR, finance, and IT jobs, boosting the state's economy by an estimated $1.11 billion through tax revenue. This win further solidifies North Carolina's growing status as a major life sciences and tech hub, attracting other giants like Eli Lilly and Apple.

feed.title: Kurt Preston Named Vice President of Sourcing for Sonos
feed.summary: Sonos has appointed Kurt Preston as its new Vice President of Sourcing. Preston brings over 20 years of global experience in procurement and operations from companies like GE, Dell, Compaq, and Apple. ON Partners, specifically Heidi Hoffman and Jamie Ganner, facilitated this successful placement.

feed.title: S&P 500 falls Friday, notches second straight week of losses in September slump
feed.summary: Stocks, including the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite, experienced declines on Friday, marking the second consecutive week of losses for the S&P 500. This downturn is attributed to investor caution regarding the resurgent Covid virus, an upcoming Federal Reserve meeting, and September's historical tendency for market weakness. The market is also anticipating increased volatility due to seasonal patterns, continued virus uncertainty, and upcoming monetary and fiscal policy announcements from September to October.

feed.title: Is a stock market correction on the horizon?
feed.summary: The S&P 500 has seen a recent dip, leading analysts to narrow predictions for the U.S. equity market. While tech stocks may face challenges due to Fed policy and rising interest rates, other sectors like energy and small-caps are showing potential. Despite concerns, corrections are normal, and many analysts remain bullish on long-term market growth, suggesting current dips could be buying opportunities.

feed.title: The computer chip industry has a dirty climate secret
feed.summary: The computer chip industry, while crucial for green technologies, has a significant and growing carbon footprint due to its high energy and water consumption and hazardous waste production. Major players like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) are beginning to address this by committing to net-zero emissions and investing in renewable energy, driven by investor pressure and demand for greener supply chains. Decarbonizing the industry will require substantial innovation in manufacturing processes, energy efficiency, and the use of less polluting materials.

feed.title: Apple’s Upgrades Were Modest, and Maybe That Was the Point
feed.summary: This article discusses Apple's recent product announcements, including the iPhone 13, new iPads, and Apple Watch Series 7. The author suggests that the upgrades were modest, potentially by design. It also touches on the uncertainty surrounding Apple's App Store reforms.

feed.title: DFAX Stock Price | Dimensional World ex U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information on the Dimensional World ex U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAX), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), and key financial metrics. It also covers the ETF's historical performance, dividend yield, expense ratio, and top holdings, offering a comprehensive overview for potential investors.

feed.title: Mattel, Warner Music Group's Arts Music Release He-Man, Hot Wheels, Thomas & Friends Albums
feed.summary: Mattel has partnered with Warner Music Group's Arts Music division to release new albums based on popular franchises like He-Man and the Masters of the Universe, Hot Wheels, and Thomas & Friends. These releases build upon an existing multi-year global music licensing agreement. The new albums feature original soundtracks and theme songs from their respective series, with additional releases planned for the future across major digital music platforms.

feed.title: Kontakt.io Launches Nano Tag, the World's Smallest Affordable, Disposable & Wearable BLE Tag
feed.summary: Kontakt.io Inc. has launched the Nano Tag, hailed as the world's smallest affordable, disposable, and wearable BLE tag. This innovation aims to address worker safety and patient/visitor experience in healthcare, hospitality, and events by offering a cost-effective and environmentally friendly solution. The Nano Tag features a compact design, a non-toxic battery, Bluetooth 5.0 technology, and is available for purchase through various channels starting from $4.99 unit price.

feed.title: รีวิวกองทุน SCBKEQTG: หุ้นเกาหลีกับศักยภาพการเป็นแถวหน้าของเอเชีย

feed.summary: บทความนี้รีวิวกองทุน SCBKEQTG ซึ่งลงทุนในหุ้นเกาหลีใต้ โดยเน้นศักยภาพทางเศรษฐกิจของประเทศจากเทคโนโลยี การส่งออก และบริษัทชั้นนำ นอกจากนี้ยังอธิบายโครงสร้างของดัชนี MSCI Korea 25/50 ที่กองทุนใช้อ้างอิง และแนะนำ 10 อันดับแรกของหุ้นที่กองทุนลงทุน ซึ่งรวมถึง Samsung Electronics และ SK Hynix ที่เป็นผู้นำด้านเทคโนโลยีระดับโลก

feed.title: SiriusXM Launches New Free Channel and Premium Podcast Subscription with Marvel Entertainment on Apple Podcasts
feed.summary: SiriusXM and Marvel Entertainment have partnered to launch Marvel Podcasts Unlimited, a premium audio subscription available exclusively on Apple Podcasts. This service offers subscribers early and exclusive access to new scripted and unscripted Marvel podcast series, including "Marvel's Wastelanders" and the Spanish-language adaptation of "Marvel's Wolverine: The Long Night." Additionally, a free Marvel channel on Apple Podcasts will feature popular existing series.

feed.title: BSX Begins Publishing MIAX Pearl Equities
feed.summary: The Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX), a subsidiary of Miami International Holdings, Inc., has started publishing MIAX Pearl Equities market data for 10 actively traded U.S. companies to the Pyth Network. This marks BSX's first initiative to provide high-fidelity data to the Pyth Network, contributing U.S. equities exchange market data to support DeFi markets. The initial companies include Apple, Amazon, Google, Netflix, and Tesla.

feed.title: BSX publishes market data to Pyth Network
feed.summary: The Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX) has started publishing MIAX Pearl Equities market data for ten US companies to the Pyth Network, a decentralized financial market data distribution platform. This initiative marks BSX's first step in providing high-fidelity data to the blockchain industry via the Solana network, with plans to expand the data breadth. The included companies are major US firms like Apple, Amazon, and Tesla, marking a significant development for DeFi markets.

feed.title: Apple bans Fortnite from App Store until appeals are exhausted
feed.summary: Apple has informed Epic Games that Fortnite will remain banned from its App Store until all appeals in their legal dispute are exhausted, a process that Epic CEO Tim Sweeney estimates could take five years. This decision comes after a recent court ruling largely favored Apple, despite requiring the company to allow developers to direct users to alternative payment systems. Epic had sought reinstatement of its developer account, promising to adhere to Apple's guidelines, but Apple cited Epic's past "duplicitous conduct" and Sweeney's critical remarks post-ruling as reasons for refusal.

feed.title: NetApp slims down to build itself back up
feed.summary: NetApp has relocated its headquarters from Sunnyvale to Santana Row in San Jose, a move celebrated as the Deal of the Year. This strategic decision involves slimming down its physical footprint to build itself back up, moving into a 301,019-square-foot lease and selling its larger 21.5-acre Sunnyvale Campus. The article highlights the rationale behind this significant real estate transaction and its implications for NetApp.

feed.title: Freshworks becomes first Indian SaaS company to list shares in US after stellar debut
feed.summary: Freshworks Inc., an Indian-origin SaaS company, made a stellar debut on Nasdaq, becoming the first of its kind to list shares in the U.S. The company raised over $1.03 billion in its IPO, with its market capitalization surging to $13 billion on the first day of trading. This achievement not only marked a significant milestone for the company and its founders but also created 500 new millionaires among its employees.

feed.title: Barry Diller's IAC Pursuing $2.5B Deal For Meredith Corp. - Report
feed.summary: Barry Diller's InterActive Corp. (IAC) is reportedly in advanced discussions to acquire magazine publisher Meredith Corp. for over $2.5 billion. Meredith halted its print publication of People magazine, among others, and is focusing on its digital growth. The deal would significantly expand IAC's digital media presence by adding many of Meredith's brands.

feed.title: Apple threatened Facebook ban over slavery posts on Instagram
feed.summary: Apple threatened to remove Facebook's products from its App Store in 2019 after a BBC investigation revealed domestic "slaves" were being sold on apps, including Instagram. The Wall Street Journal's "Facebook Files" revealed Facebook took limited action until Apple's threat, despite internal reports showing knowledge of the online slave trade prior to the BBC's inquiry. Following the BBC report and Apple's intervention, Facebook banned hashtags, removed accounts, and developed technology to proactively address content related to domestic servitude.

feed.title: Apple iPhone 13 tests whether deals, cameras will spur upgrades
feed.summary: Apple released its iPhone 13 lineup, featuring new camera technology and enhanced battery life, to assess if these improvements combined with aggressive carrier deals will drive upgrades despite a lack of dramatic redesign. U.S. carriers like Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile are offering significant incentives, sometimes even making the iPhone 13 Pro free with trade-ins, which is already leading to backlogs in online orders for the new devices. This launch kicks off a busy holiday season for Apple, with other new products like a redesigned iPad mini and upcoming MacBook Pros and AirPods also in the pipeline.

feed.title: Netflix VP Of Communications Richard Siklos Leaves The Company
feed.summary: Richard Siklos, Netflix's VP of communications since 2017, has announced his departure from the streaming giant. His exit follows other executive changes at Netflix, which has seen significant growth in recent years and has maintained its leadership in the streaming market despite increased competition. Siklos previously held a senior communications role at Time Warner and had a notable career as a journalist specializing in the media business.

feed.title: BlackBerry ditches Classic, adding to smartphone future doubts
feed.summary: BlackBerry Ltd announced it will stop producing its Classic smartphone model, a move that contributed to a more than 4% drop in stock and intensified doubts about the future of its handset business. The decision reinforces the company's shift towards software, as the Classic and other BlackBerry 10 devices struggled to compete with the iPhone and Android-powered phones. Additionally, an internal U.S. Senate memo revealed that major carriers were informed that all BlackBerry 10 devices had been discontinued.

feed.title: RIM delays new BlackBerry launch; sales crumble
feed.summary: Research In Motion Ltd (RIM) has delayed the launch of its next-generation BlackBerry phones until early 2013, a significant setback for the company facing crumbling sales, a deeper-than-expected quarterly operating loss, and deep job cuts. The delay, coupled with a 30% workforce reduction and mounting financial pressure, signals a potential terminal decline for the once-dominant technology firm. Analysts are concerned about RIM's survival as it misses crucial shopping periods and struggles to compete with Apple and Android.

feed.title: Apple claimed it had less than 20 million TV+ subscribers in July, showbiz union says
feed.summary: A showbiz union, the International Alliance of Theatrical Stage Employees (IATSE), claims Apple stated it had under 20 million Apple TV+ subscribers in the U.S. and Canada as of July 1. This allowed Apple to pay lower rates to production crews under a contract provision for streaming services with fewer than 20 million subscribers. The union is currently in negotiations for a new contract and is considering a strike, arguing that streaming platforms, despite their profitability, are still leveraging outdated contract terms.

feed.title: Apple’s power move to kneecap Facebook advertising is working
feed.summary: Apple's iOS 14.5 privacy changes, which allow users to opt-out of app tracking, are significantly harming Facebook's advertising business. Advertisers, particularly performance marketers, are struggling to measure ad effectiveness and are consequently reducing their spending on Facebook and diversifying to other platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, and email. Critics suggest Apple's move is not solely privacy-driven but also a strategic play to build its own advertising platform, as it competes directly with Facebook's messaging apps.

feed.title: Xfinity Mobile and Comcast Business Mobile to offer all-new iPhone 13 Pro, iPhone 13 Pro Max, iPhone 13, iPhone 13 mini, iPad, and iPad mini with Orders Starting Today
feed.summary: Xfinity Mobile and Comcast Business Mobile are now offering the full lineup of new Apple products, including the iPhone 13 series, iPad (9th generation), and iPad mini. Customers who port a new number to Xfinity Mobile will receive $300 off any iPhone, while existing customers upgrading their line will get a $300 Visa pre-paid card. The new devices feature advanced camera systems, the A15 Bionic chip, improved battery life, and enhanced 5G capabilities.

feed.title: Avantis U.S. Large Cap Value ETF
feed.summary: The Avantis U.S. Large Cap Value ETF (AVLV) offers investors exposure to large-cap U.S. value equities with an active management strategy. The ETF has an expense ratio of 0.15% and has shown strong performance across various timeframes compared to its category average. It holds a diversified portfolio with significant allocations to finance, retail trade, and electronic technology sectors, and its top holdings include major tech and industrial companies.

feed.title: Which Game Making Tool Is Right for You?
feed.summary: This article evaluates several popular game development tools—GameMaker Studio, RPG Maker, Unity, and Source Engine—to help aspiring indie developers choose the best one. It highlights GameMaker Studio as ideal for beginners due to its user-friendliness and gradual introduction to coding, while noting Unity's versatility for those willing to learn more complex systems. RPG Maker is recommended for those focusing on design over coding, and Source Engine is deemed better for experienced developers due to its age and complexity.
